textResponse:
Here are some of the latest AI headlines, with sources:

1. **OpenAI introduces GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini for developers**  
   OpenAI announced new API models focused on coding, instruction-following, and lower latency.  
   Source: OpenAI Blog — https://openai.com/blog/

2. **Meta releases new Llama 3 updates for open-weight AI models**  
   Meta continues expanding its Llama family, targeting both research and commercial AI use.  
   Source: Meta AI — https://ai.meta.com/blog/

3. **Google DeepMind advances Gemini models and AI agents**  
   Google has been highlighting Gemini improvements across search, productivity, and multimodal tasks.  
   Source: Google Blog / DeepMind — https://blog.google/ and https://deepmind.google/discover/blog/

4. **Anthropic expands Claude capabilities for enterprise use**  
   Anthropic has been rolling out updates around longer context windows, tool use, and business integrations.  
   Source: Anthropic News — https://www.anthropic.com/news

5. **NVIDIA unveils new AI chips and infrastructure for model training**  
   NVIDIA continues to dominate AI infrastructure headlines with new GPUs, enterprise AI platforms, and partnerships.  
   Source: NVIDIA Newsroom — https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news

6. **EU moves forward on AI regulation implementation**  
   The European Union is progressing on enforcement and implementation details for the AI Act.  
   Source: European Commission — https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/european-approach-artificial-intelligence

7. **Apple increases focus on on-device AI features**  
   Apple has been developing more privacy-focused AI capabilities for its devices and software ecosystem.  
   Source: Apple Newsroom — https://www.apple.com/newsroom/

8. **Microsoft expands Copilot across Windows and Office products**  
   Microsoft continues integrating AI assistants into productivity software and cloud services.  
   Source: Microsoft News — https://news.microsoft.com/
